text:
There once was a curious frog named Fredrick who lived at the edge of a crystal-clear pond. Fredrick adored skipping stones across the water. One sunny morning, as he prepared his precious stones, he noticed something extraordinary—a shadow darting around. Hopbing onto some gnawed-on bones, it looked like a small bat.

The bat fluttered around, exploring the air with remarkable grace. Fredrick waved his limbs nervously. The bat landed on a branch just above him. With a flicker of curiosity, Fredrick asked, "Excuse me! Do you want to come Skipping Stones with me? Sometimes they have tiny fish!"

The bat responded in a squeaky voice that could probably echo across the pond. "Yes! Yes, I would love that!" It flew down into the water, landing on Fredrick's outstretched toe.

From that day on, Fredrick embarked on countless treasure-hunting trips with his new, avian friend. They found hundreds of
